description_long: This is a two-story suburban house with a prominent gable roof and a covered front porch supported by columns. The exterior is clad in light-colored horizontal siding with stonework at the base, and the windows are symmetrically arranged with dark shutters. The front features a double garage door and a main entryway under the porch, while the rear and sides are simpler with fewer windows and utility fixtures. The roof is steeply pitched and primarily gabled, with a secondary gable over the garage. Architectural details include decorative trim, a modest overhang, and a mix of rectangular and square windows. The house is typical of modern American residential neighborhoods, designed for family living and vehicle storage.
description: A two-story suburban house with a gable roof, covered front porch, double garage, and light siding with stone accents. Symmetrical windows and decorative trim give it a modern, family-friendly appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry porch, double garage, symmetrical windows with shutters, and stonework at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long expanse of siding, utility boxes, and minimal window placement.
description_view_2: Rear view featuring the double garage door, upper windows, and the back portion of the porch roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with two windows and a clear view of the secondary gable and porch extension.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered front porch with columns, steps, and a single main door under an overhang
